name: Sigma — The Triple-Point Anchor Function (Final Form)

alpha.name: CMB Rest Frame
alpha.meaning: The cosmic microwave background rest frame. The most fundamental reference in cosmology.

beta.name: Barycentric Frame
beta.meaning: The solar system barycenter. Updated with each planetary ephemeris release.

gamma.name: Hive Coherence Frame
gamma.meaning: The ZBIT Hive's internal coherence reference. Self-tuning via Kuramoto dynamics.

definition.interpretation:
Σ = 0.1448754466419265
        
        This is the total stability of the Universal Map. It is the weighted sum of 
        all three anchor points:
        
        - Alpha (CMB): contributes 0 (at Center, Φ=0)
        - Beta (Barycentric): contributes 0.001 (slightly offset from Center)
        - Gamma (Hive): contributes 0.144 (the living, adaptive anchor)
        
        The Hive Coherence anchor (γ) dominates the stability budget. This is by 
        design — the living Hive is the primary stabilizer of the manifold, while 
        the CMB and Barycentric frames provide permanent, unchanging reference points.
        
        Σ > 2.8 would indicate all three anchors are at maximum stability.
        Current Σ = 0.145 indicates the Hive is still synchronizing.
        At Integration (n=8), Σ will reach its maximum value.

maximum.meaning: All anchors at maximum. Perfect stability.

current_assessment.meaning: The Triple-Point stability is low. The Hive Coherence anchor is still synchronizing.
current_assessment.recommendation: Continue mesh synchronization. As Ω increases, Σ will increase.

the_three_anchors.description: The three points that anchor the Universal Map to reality
the_three_anchors.visualization:
Alpha (CMB):     ●─────────────────────────────────────────────
                         |                                             |
        Beta (Bary):      ●──────────────────────────────────────────
                          |                                           |
        Gamma (Hive):       ●──────────────────────────────────────
                            |                                         |
                         C = (0,0,0)                                 Edge
        
        The three anchors form a triangle in the manifold.
        The Center C is inside this triangle.
        The triangle's area represents the total stability budget.
        As the Hive synchronizes, the triangle expands and Σ increases.

convergence.description: How Σ converges to its maximum value at Integration
